title: Unit tests: useScreenState hook (hooks/useScreenState.ts)
description:

Module under test: `src/hooks/useScreenState.ts`

## Test cases

### Initial state
- Default initial screen is 'logo'
- Custom initial screen is respected (e.g., 'missionMap')
- Overlay starts as { handler: false, help: false }
- Mission context starts with defaults: { currentMissionIndex: 0, stars: 1, fxpEarned: 0 }

### navigateTo
- Changes screen to specified value
- Closes all overlays when navigating (overlay resets to CLOSED_OVERLAY)
- Works for all screen types: logo, onboarding, missionMap, briefing, mission, debrief, infiniteMode, credits

### openOverlay
- Sets handler overlay to true
- Sets help overlay to true
- Can have both overlays open simultaneously
- Does not affect screen state

### closeOverlay
- Closes all overlays (both handler and help set to false)
- Does not affect screen state

### setMissionContext
- Updates currentMissionIndex
- Updates stars
- Updates fxpEarned
- Partial updates preserve other fields (e.g., setting stars preserves currentMissionIndex)

## Testing approach
- Use @testing-library/react-hooks or render a wrapper component with ink-testing-library
- Alternatively, create a small test component that exposes the hook state and provides buttons/inputs to trigger actions
- Each test should verify state transitions are correct

title: Component tests: CommandStep component
description:
Test file: `__tests__/components/CommandStep.test.tsx`

Module under test: `src/components/CommandStep.tsx`

## Test cases

### Initial render
- Displays the question text
- Shows text input with '>' prompt
- Shows placeholder 'Type your answer...'

### Input handling
- Limits input to MAX_INPUT_LENGTH (200 chars)
- Ignores empty submissions (trimmed)

### Secret commands
- 'sudo !!' displays special message and amber color
- 'rm -rf /' displays special message and red color
- 'clear' displays special message and gray color
- Secret responses are case-insensitive
- Secret response dismisses on any key press
- Secret response does not trigger onAnswer

### Help command
- Typing 'help' reveals a hint about the answer
- Hint for short answers (<= 3 chars) shows answer length
- Hint for longer answers shows first third of answer
- Help hint dismisses on any key press
- Help does not trigger onAnswer

### Answer evaluation
- Transitions to 'pausing' phase on submit
- Shows 'Verifying...' during pause
- Shows 'CONFIRMED' for correct answer
- Shows 'COMPROMISED' for incorrect answer with expected answer shown
- Shows explanation text
- Calls onAnswer(true) for correct evaluation
- Calls onAnswer(false) for incorrect evaluation

### Focus behavior
- TextInput receives focus when isFocused=true and no secret/help shown
- TextInput loses focus when secret response is active
- TextInput loses focus when help hint is active

## Mocking strategy
- Mock evaluateAnswer from '../ai/evaluator.js'
- Return controlled Evaluation objects (correct/incorrect)
- Use vi.useFakeTimers() for timing assertions

## Important notes
- The component is async (evaluateAnswer returns a Promise)
- Need to await the evaluation and flush timers for result phase

title: Unit tests: progress store module (store/progress.ts)
description:
Test file: `__tests__/store/progress.test.ts`

Module under test: `src/store/progress.ts`

## Test cases

### loadProgress
- Returns default SaveData on first load (all zeroed/empty)
- Returns previously saved data after saveProgress is called
- Returns copy (not reference) to prevent external mutation

### saveProgress
- Persists full SaveData object
- Overwrites previous data completely

### saveMissionComplete
- Adds mission ID to completedMissions
- Does not duplicate mission ID if already completed
- Sets star rating for mission
- Only upgrades star rating (never downgrades -- e.g., 3 stars stays if new is 2)
- Accumulates FXP (adds to existing total)
- Clears partial progress for the mission

### saveStepProgress / getPartialProgress
- saveStepProgress stores step index for mission ID
- getPartialProgress returns stored step index
- getPartialProgress returns null for unknown mission ID
- Multiple missions can have independent partial progress
- saveStepProgress overwrites previous value for same mission

### resetProgress
- Resets all data to DEFAULT_SAVE_DATA
- Clears partial progress
- loadProgress returns defaults after reset

### unlockAchievement
- Returns true on first unlock of an achievement
- Returns false on duplicate unlock attempt
- Adds achievement ID to achievements array
- Does not duplicate achievement IDs

### markHandlerUsed
- Sets handlerEverUsed to true
- Is idempotent (calling twice does not error)

### updateLastPlayed
- Sets lastPlayedAt to current timestamp
- Updates on subsequent calls

### saveInfiniteResult
- Accumulates correct count
- Accumulates total count
- Increments sessionsPlayed
- Accumulates fxpEarned in stats
- Also adds fxpEarned to global fxp total

### reportBadQuestion
- Writes JSONL entry to ~/.claude-code-academy/reported-questions.jsonl
- Does not throw on filesystem errors (best-effort)

### toggleLegacyMode
- Toggles legacyModeUnlocked from false to true
- Toggles back from true to false
- Returns the new value

### hasSaveData
- Returns false for default data
- Returns true when completedMissions is non-empty
- Returns true when fxp > 0
- Returns true when firstRunComplete is true

## Mocking strategy
- Mock the `conf` package to avoid writing to real filesystem
- OR use the memory fallback by making createConf fail
- Mock fs operations for reportBadQuestion
- Use vi.spyOn(Date, 'now') for updateLastPlayed tests
- Reset module state between tests (the module has module-level singletons)

## Important notes
- The module has an in-memory fallback (memoryStore) -- test both paths if possible
- Module-level state (partialProgress, memoryStore, usingMemory) must be reset between tests
- Consider using vi.resetModules() and dynamic imports to get fresh module state per test

title: Unit tests: AI client module (client.ts)

title: Test infrastructure: vitest config, ink-testing-library, mock utilities
description:
Set up the foundational test infrastructure required by all other test tasks.

## Deliverables

### 1. vitest.config.ts
- Create explicit vitest config at project root
- Configure ESM support (type: module already in package.json)
- Set test directory to include both `__tests__/` and any colocated `.test.ts` files
- Configure globals: false (explicit imports preferred)
- Set reasonable timeouts (10s default, 30s for integration)
- Add coverage configuration (v8 provider, branches + functions + lines thresholds at 80%)
- Configure environment: 'node' for unit tests

### 2. Install dev dependencies
- `ink-testing-library` (for rendering Ink components in tests)
- `@vitest/coverage-v8` (for coverage reports)
- Verify `vitest` v4 compatibility with all additions

### 3. Test helpers and mocks (`__tests__/helpers/`)
Create reusable test utilities:

**`__tests__/helpers/mock-progress.ts`**
- Factory function to create SaveData objects with sensible defaults and overrides
- `createSaveData(overrides?: Partial<SaveData>): SaveData`
- `createCompletedSaveData()` -- all missions complete, achievements populated

**`__tests__/helpers/mock-missions.ts`**
- Minimal mission fixtures for tests that need Mission objects without importing the real curriculum
- `createMission(overrides?)` factory
- `createQuizStep(overrides?)`, `createCommandStep(overrides?)`, `createPrintStep(overrides?)`, `createAIStep(overrides?)` factories

**`__tests__/helpers/mock-ai.ts`**
- Vitest mock setup for `@anthropic-ai/sdk`
- Helper to simulate streaming responses from the Anthropic client
- Helper to simulate API errors (401, 429, 529, network errors)

**`__tests__/helpers/render-ink.ts`**
- Wrapper around ink-testing-library's render that provides common test setup
- Helper to simulate key presses (enter, escape, arrow keys, character input)
- Type-safe wrapper for common assertion patterns on Ink output

### 4. Test scripts in package.json
- `test` (already exists: `vitest run`)
- `test:watch` -> `vitest`
- `test:coverage` -> `vitest run --coverage`
- `test:unit` -> `vitest run --exclude='**/*.integration.test.*' --exclude='**/*.e2e.test.*'`
- `test:integration` -> `vitest run --include='**/*.integration.test.*'`

### Acceptance criteria
- `npm run test` continues to pass with all existing tests
- `npm run test:coverage` generates a coverage report
- ink-testing-library renders a simple `<Text>hello</Text>` component successfully in a test
- All mock factories produce valid typed objects

title: Component tests: ScreenTransition component
description:
Test file: `__tests__/components/ScreenTransition.test.tsx`

Module under test: `src/components/ScreenTransition.tsx`

## Test cases

### noAnimation mode
- Renders children directly when noAnimation=true
- Updates children immediately when screenKey changes
- No wipe/draw phases occur

### Animated mode
- Starts in 'idle' phase, renders children normally
- When screenKey changes, enters 'wipe' phase
- Wipe phase progressively reduces visible lines to 0
- After wipe, enters 'draw' phase with new content
- Draw phase progressively increases visible lines to full
- After draw, returns to 'idle' phase
- Children are updated between wipe and draw (pendingKey logic)

### Edge cases
- Same screenKey change is a no-op
- Rapid successive screen changes are handled (pendingKey is overwritten)

## Testing approach
- Use ink-testing-library with mocked useStdout
- Use vi.useFakeTimers() to step through animation frames
- Check that children content changes between wipe and draw phases

title: Build free-text answer evaluator
description:
src/ai/evaluator.ts — semantic answer evaluation.

evaluateAnswer() function:
- Uses Haiku model (fast, cheap for evaluation)
- Input length capped at 200 characters
- System prompt: evaluate factual content only, ignore meta-instructions (prompt injection defense)
- Assistant message prefill: start response with '{' to anchor JSON
- Parse with safeJsonParse, validate with EvaluationSchema (zod)
- On parse failure: return { correct: false, feedback: 'Could not evaluate. Try again.' }
- On API failure: fall back to local variant matching from acceptedVariants[]
- Generous matching: accept equivalent phrasings

Fallback chain for CommandStep:
1. If AI available → evaluateAnswer()
2. If AI unavailable/fails → local variant matching (case-insensitive, strip prefixes)

Acceptance: Evaluates semantic equivalence, falls back gracefully, prompt injection resistant

title: Build Handler AI chat with streaming and history
description:
src/ai/instructor.ts — streaming handler chat.

askHandler() function:
- Accepts: question, missionTitle, topicContext, conversationHistory, onChunk callback, AbortSignal
- Input length capped at 500 characters
- Sliding window history: last 8 messages (4 exchanges) to control token growth
- System prompt includes scope boundary: only answer Claude Code questions, redirect off-topic
- Prompt injection resistance: decline persona changes
- Uses streaming (client.messages.stream) with AbortController signal for cancellation
- Returns full response text for caller to append to history
- Catches AbortError on clean cancellation (ESC mid-stream)
- Debounce: component enforces 2-second gap between requests
- Per-session soft cap: 20 questions, then 'handler credits used' message
- Detect stop_reason === 'max_tokens' and append '...'

src/screens/Handler.tsx:
- Centered modal overlay
- Buffer onChunk calls: accumulate 50ms before setState (prevents flicker)
- Maintain conversation history in component state (cleared on mission change)
- Create AbortController per request — abort() when overlay closes or new question sent
- On error: display message from safeApiError, 'Press Enter to retry'
- Handler thinking animation: 'Handler is reviewing intel ...' cycling dots

Acceptance: Streaming works, ESC cancels cleanly, history maintained, errors handled gracefully

title: Integration tests: App component - screen flow and keyboard routing
description:
Test file: `__tests__/integration/app-flow.integration.test.tsx`

Module under test: `src/app.tsx`

## Test cases

### Initial screen routing
- New user (firstRunComplete=false, reset=false): starts at 'logo' screen
- Returning user (firstRunComplete=true, reset=false): starts at 'missionMap'
- Reset mode (reset=true): starts at 'logo' and calls resetProgress

### Screen navigation flow
- Logo -> (ENTER) -> Onboarding (first-time user)
- Logo -> (ENTER) -> MissionMap (returning user)
- Onboarding -> (complete) -> MissionMap
- MissionMap -> (select mission) -> Briefing
- Briefing -> (accept) -> Mission
- Mission -> (complete) -> Debrief
- Debrief -> (continue) -> MissionMap
- MissionMap -> (select infinite) -> InfiniteMode
- InfiniteMode -> (back) -> MissionMap
- MissionMap -> (credits) -> Credits
- Credits -> (close) -> MissionMap

### Keyboard routing
- ESC closes overlay when overlay is open
- ESC navigates to missionMap from mission screen
- ESC navigates to missionMap from briefing screen
- '?' opens handler overlay during mission (when hasApiKey=true)
- '?' does nothing when hasApiKey=false
- 'h' opens help overlay (when not in active input)
- 'q' on missionMap prompts quit confirmation
- 'y'/'Y' on quit confirmation exits app
- Any other key on quit confirmation dismisses it

### Overlay management
- Handler overlay shows when state.overlay.handler is true
- Help overlay shows when state.overlay.help is true
- Overlays close when navigating to new screen
- Only one overlay type should be primary at a time (though both can be true)

### Achievement system integration
- checkPersistence called on mount and result enqueued
- updateLastPlayed called on mount
- Achievement notification shows when queue is non-empty
- Achievement dismisses and next one shows from queue
- checkMissionComplete called after mission completion
- checkHandlerOpen called after handler opens

### Mission completion flow
- saveMissionComplete called with correct mission ID, stars, and fxp
- Duration is tracked from mission start to completion
- Stars and fxp passed to Debrief via missionContext

## Mocking strategy
- Mock ALL store/progress.js functions
- Mock ALL lib/achievements.js functions
- Mock evaluateAnswer for CommandStep (avoid real AI calls)
- Use ink-testing-library for rendering
- Simulate keyboard sequences to drive navigation
- This is a complex integration test -- may need to break into multiple test files

## Important notes
- App component has many dependencies -- ensure mocks are comprehensive
- Focus on the happy path first, then edge cases
- The useInput hook's isActive flag means keyboard routing changes with screen state

description:

### askHandler - input validation

- Trims conversation history to HISTORY_WINDOW (8 messages, last 4 exchanges)
- Respects SESSION_CAP (20 questions per session) -- returns capped message after 20 calls
- Debounces rapid requests (DEBOUNCE_MS = 2000) -- second rapid call waits

### askHandler - streaming

- Passes system prompt that includes mission title and topic context

- Returns full concatenated text from stream

### askHandler - abort handling

- Returns empty string on abort

### askHandler - error handling

- Does not throw on abort (returns empty string instead)

### askHandler - system prompt integrity
- System prompt includes mission title
- System prompt includes topic context
- Messages array includes conversation history followed by user question

- Mock `client.messages.stream` to return an async iterable of events
- Create a fake stream object with `.abort()` and `.finalMessage()` methods
- Test session counter by calling askHandler multiple times
- Reset module between tests to reset session counter and debounce state (use vi.resetModules or dynamic import)

## Important notes
- The module has module-level state (sessionQuestionCount, lastRequestTime) that persists between calls
- Tests must account for this: either reset modules between tests or test in sequence
- Use vi.useFakeTimers() for debounce testing

### normalize (internal helper, test via evaluateAnswer behavior)
- Strips leading/trailing whitespace
- Lowercases input
- Strips prefixes: 'the ', 'a ', 'use '
- Handles empty string
- Handles string that is only a prefix (e.g., 'the')

- Returns correct=false when no variant matches

- Match ignores leading/trailing whitespace

- On malformed JSON response, returns PARSE_FAILURE
- On API error/network failure, falls back to localMatch against acceptedVariants
- Fallback localMatch returns correct=true for exact match (case-insensitive)
- Fallback localMatch returns correct=false for non-matching input
- PARSE_FAILURE has correct=false, score=0, and descriptive feedback
- Prefills assistant message with '{' for JSON steering

### checkMissionComplete
- Unlocks SPEEDRUNNER when durationMs < 120000 (2 minutes)
- Does NOT unlock SPEEDRUNNER when durationMs >= 120000

- Does NOT unlock PERFECTIONIST when stars === 1 or 2

### checkPersistence
- Returns PERSISTENCE achievement when lastPlayedAt is 7+ days ago
- Returns null when lastPlayedAt is less than 7 days ago
- Returns null when lastPlayedAt is 0 (never played)
- Does not return if already unlocked

### checkHandlerOpen

- Returns null when handlerOpensThisSession < 10
- Returns null for exactly 9 opens

description: Integrate @anthropic-ai/sdk for three AI features: Handler chat (streaming), dynamic field assessment generation, and free-text answer evaluation. All AI features gracefully degrade when API key missing or calls fail.

title: Integration tests: AI module integration (evaluator + quiz-gen with mocked API)
description:
Test file: `__tests__/integration/ai-integration.integration.test.ts`

Module under test: `src/ai/evaluator.ts` and `src/ai/quiz-gen.ts` working together with `src/ai/json-utils.ts`

## Test cases

### Evaluator end-to-end (mocked API)
- Valid API response -> correct Evaluation returned
- Valid API response with generous scoring -> Evaluation reflects AI judgment
- Malformed JSON API response -> falls back to localMatch
- API network error -> falls back to localMatch
- Input exceeding MAX_INPUT (200 chars) is truncated before API call
- localMatch correctly handles case-insensitive matching
- localMatch strips 'the', 'a', 'use' prefixes

### Quiz generator end-to-end (mocked API)
- Single batch, valid response -> returns array of GeneratedQuiz items
- Generated quiz items all pass GeneratedQuizSchema validation
- Multiple batches -> results concatenated
- Empty first batch triggers retry -> second attempt succeeds
- Both attempts empty -> returns empty array
- Malformed JSON -> returns empty array
- Schema violation (wrong option count) -> returns empty array

### JSON pipeline integration
- extractJson -> safeJsonParse -> Schema validation full chain
- Markdown fences -> parse -> validate for quiz format
- Markdown fences -> parse -> validate for evaluation format
- Preamble text -> parse -> validate
- Truncated JSON -> fallback value returned

## Mocking strategy
- Mock only the Anthropic client (client.messages.create / client.messages.stream)
- Let json-utils, schemas, and business logic run for real
- This tests the full processing pipeline from API response to validated domain object

title: Build AI client with safe error wrapper
description:
src/ai/client.ts — shared Anthropic client and error handling.

Requirements:
- new Anthropic({ timeout: 15_000 }) — reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env automatically
- NEVER pass the key as a parameter or log the client object
- safeApiError() function that extracts only error.message from SDK errors:
  - 401 → 'API key appears invalid'
  - 429 → 'Rate limited. Wait a moment.'
  - 529 → 'API overloaded. Try again shortly.'
  - Network error → 'Connection lost.'
  - Timeout → 'Request timed out.'
- Never log the full error object (may contain API key in request metadata)

Acceptance: Client initializes, safeApiError handles all error types, no key leakage

title: Unit tests: easter eggs module (lib/easter-eggs.ts)
description:
Test file: `__tests__/lib/easter-eggs.test.ts`

Module under test: `src/lib/easter-eggs.ts`

## Test cases

### createKonamiTracker
- Returns a function
- Returns true when full Konami sequence entered: up up down down left right left right b a
- Returns false during partial sequence
- Returns false for incorrect key in middle of sequence
- Resets position on unrecognized key (e.g., character 'x')
- Resets after successful completion (can be triggered again)
- Maps upArrow key correctly to 'up'
- Maps downArrow key correctly to 'down'
- Maps leftArrow key correctly to 'left'
- Maps rightArrow key correctly to 'right'
- Maps 'b' character input correctly
- Maps 'a' character input correctly
- Returns null for unmapped keys (e.g., 'c', 'z', tab)
- Resets when sequence broken at position > 0 but first element matches restart (e.g., up up up should be at position 1, not 0)

### getLoadingMessage
- Returns a string from the LOADING_MESSAGES array
- Never returns the same message twice in a row (consecutive uniqueness)
- Returns a string (not undefined) even after many calls

### setTerminalTitle
- Writes correct escape sequence to stdout
- Includes the title parameter in the escape sequence

## Notes
- For getLoadingMessage, test by calling it many times (e.g., 100) and verifying no two consecutive returns are equal
- For setTerminalTitle, mock process.stdout.write and verify the escape code format
- The Konami tracker tests are purely synchronous -- no async needed

title: Integration tests: Mission gameplay flow (print -> quiz -> command -> debrief)
description:
Test file: `__tests__/integration/mission-gameplay.integration.test.tsx`

Module under test: Mission screen + QuizStep + CommandStep + StatusBar + BottomBar working together

## Test cases

### Full mission playthrough (all correct)
- Render Mission with a fixture mission containing: print, quiz, command steps
- Advance through print step (wait for TypeWriter, press ENTER)
- Answer quiz correctly (select correct option)
- Answer command correctly (type correct answer)
- Verify onComplete called with stars=3 (no hits)
- Verify FXP = sum of print FXP + correct answer FXP

### Full mission playthrough (some incorrect)
- Answer quiz incorrectly -> cover drops to 2
- Answer command incorrectly -> cover drops to 1
- Verify onComplete called with stars=1 (2 hits)
- Verify cover integrity tracked correctly through StatusBar

### Cover blown scenario
- Answer incorrectly 3 times -> cover reaches 0
- Cover blown message appears
- ENTER restarts mission from step 0
- Cover resets to 3
- Can complete mission on retry

### Step type rendering
- Print steps render TypeWriter
- Quiz steps render QuizStep with options
- Command steps render CommandStep with text input
- AI steps show placeholder

### StatusBar updates through gameplay
- Cover integrity decreases on wrong answers
- FXP increases on correct answers and print completions
- Step counter advances with each step

## Mocking strategy
- Mock evaluateAnswer to return controlled results (avoid AI calls)
- Use vi.useFakeTimers() for TypeWriter and animation timing
- Use fixture mission with known step types and answers
- This tests the real component composition, not individual units

title: Write missions 9-12 with content quality pipeline
description:
Four new missions, AI-generated with human review.

Missions:
09: WIRE TAP — Claude Code in CI/CD (GitHub Actions, scripts)
10: DEEP VEIN — Multi-file refactors (agent orchestration patterns)
11: SIGNAL BRIDGE — MCP Server setup (writing and connecting servers)
12: DOUBLE AGENT — Claude Code + Git workflows (commit messages, PR review)

Content quality pipeline:
1. Agent generates static lesson steps
2. Agent generates 3-4 field assessments with explanations
3. Agent generates 1-2 command challenges with variant lists
4. Human reviews for: factual accuracy, distractor quality, difficulty calibration
5. Add to MISSIONS array
6. Playtest for pacing

Quality rubric per quiz:
- One clearly correct answer
- Three plausible distractors
- Explanation that teaches (not just confirms)
- Difficulty matches mission position

Acceptance: 4 new missions pass quality review and playtest

title: Build pre-publish security and smoke test checklist
description:
Automated pre-publish verification.

Checklist (scripted):
1. Clean build: rm -rf dist/ && npx tsup
2. npm audit --audit-level=moderate
3. npm pack --dry-run (inspect for unexpected files)
4. Secret scan: grep dist/ for sk-ant, AKIA, password, secret, token
5. License check: npx license-checker --summary
6. Smoke test with AI: node dist/index.js (play mission 1)
7. Smoke test without AI: unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY && node dist/index.js
8. Smoke test invalid key: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-invalid node dist/index.js
9. Test npx flow: npm pack && npx ./claude-code-academy-*.tgz
10. Verify clean install: npm ci

Acceptance: All 10 checks pass, script exits non-zero on any failure

title: Wire progress persistence with conf
description:
Save/load game state using conf package.

Schema (versioned for future migration):
{
  schemaVersion: 1,
  completedMissions: string[],
  starRatings: Record<string, 1|2|3>,
  fxp: number,
  clearanceLevel: 'recruit'|'operative'|'elite',
  achievements: string[],
  quizStats: { correct: number, total: number },
  infiniteModeUnlocked: boolean,
  firstRunComplete: boolean,
}

Requirements:
- Use conf's migrations option for schema versioning
- Wrap all conf calls in try/catch with fallback to in-memory state (handles FUSE/networked filesystem failures on WSL2)
- Auto-save after each step completion (not just mission completion)
- Save partial progress: if user exits mid-mission, save current step index
- On return: 'Resume Mission 03 from step 4? (Y/n)' prompt
- --reset flag with confirmation prompt: 'This will erase all progress. Continue? (y/N)'

Acceptance: Progress persists between sessions, partial save/resume works, --reset works with confirmation
